  "description": "<h4 id="about-the-challenge" class="h2-title">About the challenge</h4>\n<p>AI that doesn't just provide answers—it helps you take action.</p>\n<p>Building Agents for Real-World Challenges is your opportunity to move beyond the chatbot and into the world of <strong>agents that accomplish tasks for you</strong>. This hackathon is designed for every level of technical builder: whether you are writing your first script or architecting complex systems, this is your chance to build an <strong>agent</strong> that can reason, plan, and execute tasks under your oversight.</p>\n<p><strong>Built with Gemini’s</strong> advanced reasoning and our technology partners' solutions, you will create agents that <strong>help people get more done</strong>. You provide the logic; Gemini provides the brain; our partners provide the <strong>superpowers</strong>.</p>\n<p>Turn your ideas into agents that solve real-world problems.</p>\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\n<h4 id="partners" class="h2-title">Partners</h4>\n<p>Three partner tracks are now live. Pick one and build with their MCP server:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><a href="https://rapid-agent.devpost.com/details/arize-resources" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Arize</a></li>\n<li><a href="https://rapid-agent.devpost.com/details/elastic-resources" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Elastic</a></li>\n<li><a href="https://rapid-agent.devpost.com/details/fivetran-resources" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Fivetran</a></li>\n<li>And more partners to be announced!</li>\n</ul>\n<h5 id="the-partner-bucket-system">The "Partner Bucket" System</h5>\n<p>There are three identical prize buckets—one for each of our featured partner tracks. Instead of everyone competing for one giant pile of money, you are competing within the specific "bucket" of the partner technology you used.</p>\n<p>Prize Tier Amount (Per Partner Bucket):🥇 <strong>1st Place</strong> $5,000🥈 <strong>2nd Place</strong> $3,000🥉 <strong>3rd Place</strong> $2,000</p>\n<h5 id="how-to-qualify">How to Qualify</h5>\n<ol>\n<li>\n<strong>Pick a Partner</strong>: Choose the partner technology that best fits the mission you want to accomplish.</li>\n<li>\n<strong>Build Your Agent</strong>: Create your agent—built with Gemini 3—using Google Cloud Agent Builder.                 <section class="large-12 columns" role="main">
                  <h5>About Arize</h5>
<p>Arize is the single platform built to help you accelerate development of AI apps and agents – then perfect them in production. Arize AX is an AI engineering platform focused on evaluation and observability. It helps AI engineers and AI product managers develop, evaluate, iterate and observe and monitor AI applications and agents. Arize helps enterprises increase their speed in building AI agents and ensure effectiveness for those outcomes that they can trust in production environments.</p>
<h5>Build Gemini Agents with Full Observability and Self-Introspection via MCP</h5>
<p>Ship agents that do more than run.  Ship agents that can self improve. With Arize Phoenix, your Gemini-powered agent gets production-grade tracing from day one, plus the ability to query its own traces, prompts, datasets, and experiments as tools at runtime via the Phoenix MCP server. Every decision your agent makes becomes inspectable, evaluable, and improvable.</p>
<p><strong>We'll evaluate submissions based on technical implementation, meaningful use of tracing and MCP, quality of the agent's self-improvement loop, and overall impact</strong>.</p>
<p>Here are some guidelines to get you started:</p>
<ul>
<li>The Arize track <strong>requires a code-owned agent </strong>runtime — Gemini CLI, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform SDK, Google ADK, Agent Runtime, or Cloud Run. The visual Agent Builder alone is not supported for tracing integration. You must be able to instrument your code directly.</li>
<li>Instrument your agent with OpenInference. Auto-instrumentors exist for Google ADK, Agent Platform, Google GenAI, LangChain, LlamaIndex and many other frameworks.</li>
<li>Send traces to Phoenix Cloud (free SAAS) or self-hosted Phoenix</li>
<li>Configure the Phoenix MCP server in your agent so it can introspect its own operational data at runtime</li>
<li>Run evaluations on your traces with LLM-as-a-Judge or code evals to demonstrate quality</li>
<li>Bonus points for agents that use their own observability data to improve over time</li>
</ul>
<h5>How do I get started?</h5>
<p>The fastest path is a free <a href="https://app.phoenix.arize.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Phoenix Cloud</a> account. Grab your API key, pip install an OpenInference instrumentor, and you're tracing in under five minutes. Phoenix is fully open-source, so you can also <a href="https://github.com/Arize-ai/phoenix" target="_blank" rel="noopener">self-host</a> if you prefer.</p>
<p>For the MCP integration, @arizeai/phoenix-mcp runs via npx and drops into any MCP client config — including Gemini CLI's settings.json.</p>
<h5>Resources</h5>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://app.phoenix.arize.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Phoenix Cloud</a> — Free tier, hosted Phoenix</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/Arize-ai/phoenix" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Phoenix on GitHub</a> — Open-source, self-hostable</li>
<li><a href="https://arize.com/docs/phoenix" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Phoenix documentation</a> — Tracing, evals, datasets, experiments, prompts</li>
<li><a href="https://arize.com/docs/phoenix/integrations/phoenix-mcp-server" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Phoenix MCP Server guide</a> — Runtime introspection via MCP</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/Arize-ai/openinference" target="_blank" rel="noopener">OpenInference on GitHub</a> — OpenTelemetry-compatible auto-instrumentors and utilities</li>
</ul>
<h5>Instrumentors for Gemini / Agent Platform / ADK:</h5>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://pypi.org/project/openinference-instrumentation-google-adk/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">openinference-instrumentation-google-adk</a> — For Google ADK agents</li>
<li><a href="https://pypi.org/project/openinference-instrumentation-vertexai/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">openinference-instrumentation-vertexai</a> — For Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform SDK and Gemini via generative_models</li>
<li><a href="https://github.com/Arize-ai/openinference/tree/main/python/instrumentation/openinference-instrumentation-google-genai" target="_blank" rel="noopener">openinference-instrumentation-google-genai</a> — For the unified google-genai SDK</li>
</ul>
<h5>Quickstarts: get up and running fast</h5>
<ul>
<li><em>[PLACEHOLDER — Arize hackathon starter repo URL]</em> — End-to-end example: traced Gemini agent + Phoenix MCP + evaluations</li>
<li><a href="https://docs.arize.com/arize/llm-tracing/tracing-integrations-auto/vertex-ai-gemini" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Agent Platform (Gemini) tracing guide</a> — Step-by-step setup</li>
<li><a href="https://arize.com/docs/phoenix/evaluation/llm-evals" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Phoenix LLM-as-a-Judge evals</a> — Add evaluation pipelines to your submission</li>
</ul>
<h5>Connect with Arize</h5>
<ul>

<li>Technical questions during the hackathon: Richard Young — ryoung@arize.com</li>
</ul>
